symptoms.symptom_name: Loss of consciousness after high-energy head trauma

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Defining feature of significant head injury.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Traumatic brain injury with altered consciousness

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Suggests an ongoing/expanding process rather than a stable injury.

symptoms.symptom_name: Anisocoria and Cushing's reflex (hypertension with relative bradycardia)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Uncal herniation from raised intracranial pressure
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Clinician-only findings sealed from telehealth chat.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Traumatic brain injury with expanding intracranial haemorrhage
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Loss of consciousness, repeated vomiting, amnesia, and worsening headache together confirm a neurosurgical emergency.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Immediate 000 dispatch for CT head imaging and neurosurgical evaluation
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Time-critical; expanding intracranial bleeds progress to fatal herniation without urgent intervention.
Confirms presence, type, and extent of intracranial haemorrhage, but should not delay emergency dispatch.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Driving the patient to hospital privately without spinal precautions
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Risks worsening a possible spinal injury and delays paramedic-level airway/neurological monitoring.
